Off Calendar Feb-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: LAWRENCE MURRAY, ESQ., NAD ROBERT STRICKLAND, ESQ., 415.673.0555, FOR PLAINTIFFS HEZZACK DANIELS, TIMON HOWARD, DARRELL HUNTER, JAMES JOHNSON, HOPE DORTON, ROBERT DORTON, SHANIEQUA DORTON AND A'BLAZA DORTON.
ROD FLIEGEL, ESQ., KAI-CHING CHA, ESQ., AND ALISON HIGHTOWER, ESQ., 415.433.1940, FOR DEFENDANTS RECOLOGY INC. AND RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: PLAINTIFFS HEZZACK DANIELS, TIMON HOWARD, DARRELL HUNTER, JAMES JOHNSON, HOPE DORTON, ROBERT DORTON, SHANIEQUA DORTON, A'BLAZA DORTON'S Notice Of Renewed Motion For Class Certification is Granted in part. Two classes are certified. The first certified class is represented by Mr. Daniels and consists of all non-DOT regulated employees who have held the job title of classifier at the Pier 96 facility at anytime from November 1, 2009 to the present and for at least some portion of their employment during that time period they were members of Local 350. The second certified class is represented by Mr. Hunter and Mr. Smith and consists of all non-DOT regulated employees who have held the job title of material handler at the 501 Tunnel Avenue facility at anytime from November 1, 2009 to the present and for at least some portion of their employment during that time period they were members of Local 350. Both certified classes are certified only for the following claims alleged in the first amended complaint: 1) the fourth cause of action for violation of section 17200; 2) the portion of the seventh cause of action alleging invasion of privacy (but not discrimination) for violation of the California Constitution and the San Francisco Police Code; 3) the ninth cause of action for violation of the FEHA, the California Constitution and the San Francisco Police Code; 4) the thirteenth cause of action for violation of the Bane Act; and 5) the fourteenth cause of action for declaratory and injunctive relief. All other portions of the motion are denied. defendant's request for 60 day stay of case pending appellate review is granted. defendant's request for statement of decision is denied. counsel for both parties is ordered to submit proposed orders to the court for signature. Judge: HAROLD KAHN, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS RECOLOGY INC., and RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to the 10th cause of action for failure to prevent violation of FEHA. The claim fails only where it stands alone, and plaintiffs can pursue this theory of liability if they can establish the underlying wrong, e.g., discrimination. See Trujillo v. N. County Transit Dist., 63 Cal.App.4th 280, 287-89. The Court sustains without leave to amend the demurrer to the 7th cause of action for invasion of privacy as to the Dorton plaintiffs. The invasion of privacy cause of action did not survive decedent's death. See Flynn v. Highman (1983) 149 Cal.App.3d 677, 683. The Court overrules the demurrer to the 4th and 14th causes of action as to the Dorton plaintiffs because it appears that these causes of action are not based solely upon the invasion of privacy. A party cannot demur to part of a cause of action. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne as follows: PLAINTIFFS DISPUTE RECEIPT OF THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, BUT HAVE NOW FILED RESPONSES WHICH PRINCIPALLY CONSIST OF DENIALS AND A RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 WHICH PURPORTS TO APPLY TO EACH SEPARATE DENIAL. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFFS ARE EACH ORDERED TO SEPARATELY RESPOND TO EACH REQUEST FOR ADMISSION, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, AND, TO THE EXTENT THAT THE RESPONSE IS ANYTHING OTHER AN UNQUALIFIED ADMISSION, HE WILL PROVIDE THE DETAILED INFORMATION REQUESTED BY FORM INTERROGATORY AS TO EACH SUCH DENIAL. SUCH RESPONSES SHALL BE SERVED NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 1 2012. PLAINTIFFS AND THEIR COUNSEL WILL PAY TO DEFENDANT RECOLOGY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,500.00 BY december 1, 2012. NOTHING IN THIS ORDER SHALL BE CONsTRUED AS PERMITTING ANY PLAINTIFF TO WITHDRAW OR AMEND AN ADMISSION MADE IN RESPONSE TO A REQUEST FOR ADMISSION WITHOUT FIRST COMPLYING WITH CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2033.300(A). Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella; Court Reporter: angie diner 581.
